input.prompt: I need a template for creating gRPC services in .NET. Search for available gRPC templates — both installed locally and on NuGet.org. Tell me which package to install.

data.content: I need a template for creating gRPC services in .NET. Search for available gRPC templates — both installed locally and on NuGet.org. Tell me which package to install.
data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-01T00:55:30.242Z</current_datetime>

I need a template for creating gRPC services in .NET. Search for available gRPC templates — both installed locally and on NuGet.org. Tell me which package to install.

input.initialPrompt: I need a template for creating gRPC services in .NET. Search for available gRPC templates — both installed locally and on NuGet.org. Tell me which package to install.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Search for gRPC templates locally and on NuGet

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Search for gRPC templates locally and on NuGet

data.reasoningText: The user wants to find gRPC templates for .NET. I'll search for available templates both locally installed and on NuGet.org.

arguments.description: Search for gRPC templates locally and on NuGet

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "dotnet new search grpc 2>/dev/null | head -40", "description": "Search for gRPC templates locally and on NuGet", "initial_wait": 15}

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "dotnet new list grpc 2>/dev/null", "description": "List locally installed gRPC templates", "initial_wait": 15}

toolArgs.description: Search for gRPC templates locally and on NuGet

data.content:
You already have the built-in **ASP.NET Core gRPC Service** template installed locally:

```
Template:    ASP.NET Core gRPC Service
Short Name:  grpc
Language:    C#
```

**No additional package needed!** Just run:

```bash
dotnet new grpc -n MyGrpcService
```

This is the official Microsoft template that ships with the .NET SDK. It scaffolds a gRPC service with a `.proto` file, a generated service implementation, and the necessary `Grpc.AspNetCore` dependencies.

If you need something more opinionated (clean architecture, onion architecture, etc.), the NuGet.org search above lists community packages you can install with `dotnet new install <package>`.
